title: Reduced pressure therapy systems and methods including an antenna
abstract: Embodiments of negative pressure wound therapy systems and methods are disclosed. In some embodiments, an apparatus includes a housing enclosing a source of negative pressure and a controller configured to operate the source of negative pressure to provide negative pressure wound therapy to a wound. The housing can also include a communications controller configured to wirelessly transmit and receive data using a communications antenna positioned on an antenna board. The antenna board can be mounted to a communications board that include the communications controller. The antenna board can be electrically connected to the communications board via a single connector on the communications board. The antenna's ground plane can be positioned on the communications board.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED: 1 . An apparatus for applying negative pressure to a wound, the apparatus comprising: a housing; a negative pressure source positioned within the housing and configured to provide negative pressure via a fluid flow path to a wound dressing; a communications board positioned within the housing, the communications board including a controiier configured to transmit and receive data from a remote electronic device; and an antenna board positioned within the housing and mechanically mounted to the communications board and electrically connected to the communications board, the antenna board including an antenna electrically coupled to the controller, the antenna configured to wireiessiy transmit and receive signals for the controller, the antenna including a conductive area located on the antenna board and a ground area located on the communications board, 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , wherein the conductive area comprises a conductive trace, and the ground area comprises a ground plane. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the ground plane of the antenna is connected to a ground plane of the communications board. 4. The apparatus of claim 3, wherein the ground plane of the antenna is electrically connected to the ground plane of the communications board via a shunt. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the ground trace is divided into a first portion and a second portion, and the first portion is electrically connected to the shunt, a length of the first portion controlling a first bandwidth of the antenna. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein a length of the second portion controls a second bandwidth of the antenna, the second bandwidth being different from the first bandwidth. 7. The apparatus of any one or more of claims 2-6, wherein the conductive trace comprises first and second conductive trace portions configured to receive and transmit signals in high and narrow bands. 8. The apparatus of claim 7, wherein the first conductive trace portion associated with the high band has a greater surface area than the second conductive trance portion associated with the narrow band. 9. The apparatus of any or one more of claims 1 -8, wherein the antenna board is electrically connected to the communications board via a single antenna connector on the communications board. 10. The apparatus of claim 9, wherein the antenna connector on the communications board comprises a protrusion electrically connected to the antenna board via a hole in the antenna board. 1 1. The apparatus of any one or more of claims 9-10, wherein the antenna connector provides a connection for signal feed and ground. 12. The apparatus of any one or more of claims 1 -1 1 , wherein the antenna board is a printed circuit board. 13. The apparatus of any one or more of claims 1 -12, wherein the antenna is a planar inverted F-antenna. 14. The apparatus of any one or more of claims 1 -13, wherein the antenna is a dual-band cellular antenna. 15. The apparatus of any one or more of claims 1 -14, wherein the antenna board is positioned so that the conductive area faces away from the communications board. 16. The apparatus of any one or more of claims 1 -15, further comprising a canister configured to store at least some fluid removed from the wound, wherein the antenna board is positioned so that the conductive area faces the canister. 17. A method of operating, using, or manufacturing the apparatus of any one or more preceding claims.
